behaviour: FR-1: - **FR-1**: Routing picks exactly one of the 9 outcomes per user message and narrates the pick in the paqad voice.

FR-2: - **FR-2**: A `resolveRoutedWorkflow()` normalizer maps every `ClassificationWorkflow` value (and null) to exactly one of the 9 outcomes; all code-change intents map to `feature-development`; small talk and non-code content intents map to `no-workflow`.
FR-3: - **FR-3**: Rules (manifest plus text) reach the model only on the `feature-development` route; other outcomes load no rules.
FR-4: - **FR-4**: Rule-scripts execute only on the `feature-development` route, at the checks stage; no other outcome triggers them.
FR-5: - **FR-5**: The lane (fast, graduated, full) is chosen only for `feature-development`; other outcomes get no lane and show no lane line.
FR-6: - **FR-6**: All 8 real outcomes use RAG retrieval when `rag_enabled`, scoped by outcome; `no-workflow` retrieves nothing; retrieval never blocks the prompt path.
FR-7: - **FR-7**: The chosen outcome is recorded per-session as evidence, and a paused-workflow stack preserves each paused outcome's resume anchors (change key, lane, frozen-spec id).

FR-8: - **FR-8**: The framework bootstrap states the route-first order, the rules-only-for-feature-development load rule, and the per-message pause and resume rule; no provider entry file is modified.

acceptance_criteria.given: paqad is on and a plain question
acceptance_criteria.then: the outcome is project-question and no rules load and no rule-scripts run (proof: automated)

acceptance_criteria.given: an active feature-development change with recorded plan and spec
acceptance_criteria.when: the user asks a question mid-way
acceptance_criteria.then: the message routes to project-question and the feature-development plan and spec and lane and stage progress are not lost or reset (proof: automated)
paqad resumes the same change at its recorded stage and reloads its rules and does not re-plan or re-freeze (proof: automated)

acceptance_criteria.given: a new code request during a question detour
acceptance_criteria.then: paqad starts a new feature-development change rather than resuming the paused one, and an ambiguous resume asks (proof: automated)

acceptance_criteria.given: a code-change request including fix or refactor phrasing
acceptance_criteria.then: the outcome is feature-development and rules load and a lane is picked and rule-scripts run at checks (proof: automated)

acceptance_criteria.given: a prompt with a Jira or GitHub URL or ticket reference
acceptance_criteria.then: the content is read first and the outcome is chosen from that content (proof: manual)

acceptance_criteria.given: an ambiguous prompt between two real outcomes
acceptance_criteria.then: the model asks the user and offers no-workflow (proof: manual)

acceptance_criteria.given: a pentest or design-test or docs or RCA or rules-analyze request
acceptance_criteria.then: it routes correctly and uses RAG if enabled and loads no rules and runs no rule-scripts (proof: automated)
nothing loads and no RAG runs and the model just replies (proof: automated)
no entry file is modified (proof: automated)
the outcome is recorded as evidence the same way the lane is recorded today (proof: automated)
full CI is green including branch coverage at or above 95 percent and the Windows job (proof: automated)

invariants.statement: The feature-development plan-and-spec-before-code gate is never weakened or removed, and the `isFeatureDevEdit` scope predicate is unchanged.
No `CLASSIFICATION_WORKFLOWS` union member is deleted; the fold is additive plus a normalizer only.
Rule-scripts execute only on the feature-development route.
RAG retrieval never blocks the prompt path, and rag-disabled or no-workflow emits nothing, byte-identical to today.
No entry file is modified; routing logic lives only in the framework bootstrap plus the hook and pipeline layer.
The session-context artifact remains the authoritative rule contract and keeps its fail-safe marker behavior; only when the rule slice is injected up front changes.
Branch coverage stays at or above 95 percent globally and 100 percent on `src/stage-evidence`.
